name: PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop
name_original: Sierra de la Guàrdia — circular por el Castillo de Claramunt
description: This difficult 4.9-mile (7.9 km) jogging loop offers 700 feet (213 metres) of elevation gain and passes by Claramunt Castle.
region_intro: Hiking around La Pobla De Claramunt, located in the Anoia region of Catalonia, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The area features mountains and dense forests, characteristic of a Mediterranean climate with pine forests and cultivated areas. The Anoia River flows through the region, creating varied terrain from river valleys to mountain ridges. Prominent natural landmarks include the Els Tres Mollons mountain and the historic Claramunt Castle. Best hiking trails around La Pobla De Claramunt The most popular hiking route is Esgavellats Entrance – Can Titó Bridge loop from Vilanova del Camí, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied terrain near the Anoia River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Church of Santa María – Claramunt Castle loop from la Pobla de Claramunt, an easy 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This trail explores historical sites and offers views of Claramunt Castle. Local hikers also love the Prehistoric Park of Capellades loop from Capellades, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through natural landscapes and historical areas, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around La Pobla De Claramunt is defined by mountains, dense forests, and river valleys, with historical sites like Claramunt Castle integrated into many routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging trails. The routes in La Pobla De Claramunt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 550 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Claramunt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Pobla de Claramunt?
A: There are over 200 hiking routes in La Pobla de Claramunt, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 68 easy trails, 126 moderate routes, and 24 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Pobla de Claramunt?
A: The terrain in La Pobla de Claramunt is quite diverse, ranging from river valleys along the Anoia River to mountain ridges and dense Mediterranean pine forests. You'll encounter a mix of track roads, aromatic garrigue trails, and areas with dry-stone constructions. Some routes also feature significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in La Pobla de Claramunt?
A: Yes, La Pobla de Claramunt offers several easy hikes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María – Claramunt Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4 km path that explores historical sites and provides views of the castle. Another good option is "El Camí dels Pous," which offers access to recreational areas.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in La Pobla de Claramunt are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6956499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esgavellats Entrance – Can Titó Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.3 km route traversing varied terrain near the Anoia River. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5332297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Cross of Els 3 Mollons – Les Planes loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: A central attraction is the Claramunt Castle, perched atop a hill, which is integrated into many routes. You can explore its historic ruins and enjoy panoramic views. The old town of La Pobla de Claramunt also features public washhouses and engraved facades. For impressive natural formations, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4009479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap de Mort rock formation&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or natural features worth seeing?
A: Hikers can enjoy spectacular views of the Conca d'Òdena and the Montserrat mountains from various points. The Els Tres Mollons mountain offers panoramic views, and you can find numerous natural springs hidden within the mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miranda of Santa Magdalena&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Pobla de Claramunt?
A: The trails in La Pobla de Claramunt are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 550 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-marked paths, and the combination of natural beauty with historical sites like Claramunt Castle.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around La Pobla de Claramunt, particularly near popular trailheads and attractions like Claramunt Castle. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for designated parking areas.

Q: Can I reach La Pobla de Claramunt by public transport for hiking?
A: La Pobla de Claramunt is accessible by public transport, which can be a convenient option for hikers. For detailed information on routes and schedules, it's best to consult local public transport providers or use online journey planners.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are challenging trails that demand greater physical endurance. Some routes, like "La Ruta Circular" around the Anoia River, offer incredible natural views and significant elevation changes. There are also longer routes, some extending over 49 km or 71 km, such as the "Travessa Ariadna Bonell."

Q: Are permits required for hiking in La Pobla de Claramunt?
A: Generally, permits are not required for hiking on the established trails in La Pobla de Claramunt. However, if you plan to venture into specific protected areas or private lands, it's always wise to check for any local regulations or access requirements beforehand.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to the Montserrat Natural Park?
A: While La Pobla de Claramunt is conveniently close to the Montserrat Natural Park, the trails within this guide focus on the immediate vicinity of La Pobla. However, you can find routes that offer views of the Montserrat mountains, and the park itself offers additional diverse hiking alternatives with impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Summit&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Pujol de la Guàrdia is the highest mountain in the municipality of Vilanova del Camí. It has an altitude of 488 meters and offers panoramic views of the Ódena Basin. It's worth getting there!!
From the path/lane that goes in the direction of the castle you can see how it is located at the top of a hill. It looks like a postcard from afar.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop?
faqs.answer: This trail is an extreme challenge. While the exact jogging duration will vary greatly depending on your fitness level, the estimated hiking duration for the full 65.1 km (40.4 miles) loop is approximately 16 hours and 31 minutes. Experienced trail runners attempting to jog the entire route should expect a significant physical undertaking.

faqs.question: Is the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginners, especially for jogging. RouteYou rates its difficulty as 'Extreme (93/100)'. With a total ascent of 2,098 meters (about 6,883 feet) over 65.1 kilometers, it requires significant preparation, experience, and physical endurance.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly natural and rugged. A substantial 87% of the path is car-free, and most of it is unpaved. This provides a natural surface but also contributes to the 'extreme' difficulty due to elevation changes and uneven ground.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop winds through an almost untouched natural environment, intentionally avoiding urban areas. You can admire historical landmarks such as Montbuy Castle and the impressive Claramunt Castle. There are also scenic spots like Pujol de la Guàrdia and Views of Castell de Claramunt offering panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop?
faqs.answer: Given its extreme length and elevation, spring and autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, and winter might present challenges with colder weather or potential snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural environments, specific dog policies for the entire 65.1 km route are not explicitly stated. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near historical sites, and to carry out all waste. Check with local authorities for any specific regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop as it traverses public natural areas. However, if you plan to visit specific historical sites like castles along the way, they might have their own entrance fees or operating hours.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the PR-C 119 Conca d'Òdena Loop?
faqs.answer: The PR-C 119 is a long loop trail, meaning there are multiple potential access points. While a specific main trailhead parking is not detailed, you would typically look for parking in towns or villages that the loop passes through, such as near Igualada, or at designated recreational areas along the route. Always ensure parking is legal and safe.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the PC-C 119 Volta a la Conca d'Òdena (67% of the route), El Camí IT6TR6 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat (61%), and the E10 (57%). It also crosses parts of the TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània (57%) and the E09 (10%).

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail intentionally avoids urban areas, focusing on an 'almost untouched natural environment.' Therefore, direct amenities on the trail itself are scarce. For cafes, pubs, or accommodation, you would need to venture into nearby towns or villages, such as Igualada, which is a larger hub in the Conca d'Òdena region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging this extreme trail?
faqs.answer: For an extreme trail of this length and difficulty, essential gear includes sufficient water and nutrition, appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, layers of clothing suitable for changing weather, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (GPS device or app with offline maps), a headlamp if starting early or finishing late, and a fully charged phone. Given its remote nature, be prepared for self-sufficiency.
